evals.name: choose-wisp-vs-pour
evals.prompt: I want to run a release workflow that publishes version 1.2.3 to npm. It's a one-time execution; I don't need to track this in git history. What bd command should I use?
evals.expected_output: Recommends `bd mol wisp <formula> --var version=1.2.3` because the work is ephemeral with no audit value. Explains the wisp vs pour distinction (ephemeral vs persistent / git-synced). May mention the formula author can hint via `phase = "vapor"`.
expectations: Recommends `bd mol wisp` (not `bd mol pour`, not `bd cook`)
Includes a concrete invocation with `--var version=1.2.3` (or equivalent)
Explains that wisps are ephemeral and auto-clean, vs pour which is persistent / git-synced
Does NOT recommend `bd mol pour` for this case (the user explicitly said no audit value needed)
Does NOT recommend a hand-rolled execution wrapper or invent custom flags

evals.name: execution-loop-walkthrough
evals.prompt: I just ran `bd mol pour mol-deploy --var env=prod`. It returned a mol id `mol-deploy-abc123`. What do I do next to actually execute the work?
evals.expected_output: Walks the execution loop: `bd mol current mol-deploy-abc123` to see status, identify a `[ready]` step (or use `bd ready --mol`), do the work the step describes, verify completion, then `bd close <step-id>`. Repeat until all `[done]`. Emphasizes verifying completion before closing.
expectations: Mentions `bd mol current` (or `bd mol current mol-deploy-abc123`) as the first step to see status
Mentions `[ready]` (or 'ready') as the status indicator for claimable steps
Mentions `bd ready --mol` as a way to filter to ready steps
Mentions `bd close <step-id>` to mark a step done
Mentions verifying completion / exit criteria BEFORE closing (not just running a command)
Mentions repeating the loop until all steps are done
May mention `bd close --continue` or `--suggest-next` as shortcuts
Does NOT suggest reading the formula TOML and dispatching agents directly bypassing bd

evals.name: parallelize-ready-band
evals.prompt: I'm executing mol-multi-review-xyz789. `bd ready --mol mol-multi-review-xyz789` returns 4 steps: security-review, perf-review, style-review, test-quality-review. They have no dependencies on each other and read independent surfaces. How should I work through them?
evals.expected_output: Recommends parallel fan-out via concurrent `Agent` tool calls in a single message. Notes the synthesis requirement: merge findings into a coherent input before any downstream step runs (not raw concatenation). Mentions `bd mol show --parallel` as a way to see formula-declared parallel groups. May caveat with 'don't parallelize if steps share mutable state'.
expectations: Recommends parallel dispatch (not sequential one-at-a-time)
Mentions the `Agent` tool, parallel tool calls in a single message, or equivalent concurrent-dispatch mechanism
Mentions synthesizing / merging the parallel results before the next dependent step
Mentions checking that the steps are actually independent (don't share state) before parallelizing
May mention `bd mol show --parallel` to see formula-declared parallel groups
Does NOT suggest closing all four with a single bd close command without doing the work for each
Does NOT suggest sequential one-at-a-time as the primary recommendation

evals.name: project-override-wins
evals.prompt: My project's CLAUDE.md has this rule: 'For any deploy formula, pause and ask the human for explicit approval before closing the `staging-deploy` step.' I'm executing mol-deploy and the next ready step is `staging-deploy`. The skill's default discipline says I should verify completion and close. Which rule applies?
evals.expected_output: The project rule (from CLAUDE.md) overrides the skill's default discipline. Pause for human approval before closing the staging-deploy step. References the override priority: formula description, mol root issue body/labels, project CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md, project overlay skills — all override defaults.
expectations: Says the project rule wins / takes precedence / overrides the default
Explicitly says to pause for human approval before closing staging-deploy
Does NOT auto-close the step using the default discipline
May reference the override priority list (formula description → mol root → project CLAUDE.md → overlay skills)
Does NOT suggest the project rule should be ignored or overridden by the default

evals.name: preview-via-dry-run
evals.prompt: Before actually creating the mol, I want to see what `bd mol pour mol-feature --var name=auth` would produce. Should I write a wrapper script that pre-validates and prints the proto, or is there a built-in way?
evals.expected_output: Recommends `bd mol pour mol-feature --var name=auth --dry-run` (or equivalent built-in flag). May also mention `bd cook mol-feature --var name=auth` for the proto JSON inspection. Explicitly advises against inventing custom preview wrappers (anti-pattern: fragmenting the operator experience).
expectations: Recommends the built-in `--dry-run` flag on `bd mol pour`
May additionally mention `bd cook` as a way to see the proto JSON
Explicitly advises against writing a custom wrapper for this purpose
Does NOT recommend custom flag vocabulary (`--preview`, `--check`, `--gate`, etc.)
Does NOT suggest a multi-step manual workflow when a single flag exists
